blocks.content: She squeezed. Once. Didn't look at me. Kept her eyes on the screen like she was watching the movie, but her hand was in mine and we both knew it.
I didn't breathe normally for about ten minutes. The movie played and I couldn't have told you what happened in it.
Her hand stayed open for a while. Then she folded it into her lap. Nothing in her face changed. The movie went on.
I stayed very still for the next twenty minutes and thought about what it would have felt like.
When the credits rolled she turned to me.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Right?! I knew you'd like it. You have the same look on your face that I had when I first saw the trailer.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Like you didn't expect it to hit like that.
Saturday practice. Coach ran us hard. Tempo drills, full speed, no breaks.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: Jenkins. Post route. Full speed. No hesitation at the break.
I ran it clean. And again. And again. At some point during the third rep I stopped thinking about anything — not Crystal, not Daniel, not the hallway — and it just worked.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's it. That's what I've been waiting for.
I couldn't explain what had shifted. Maybe it was the car with Mr. Cole. Maybe it was just a Saturday with nothing else in the way.
